name: msg-arm
description: SessionStart + UserPromptSubmit hook — phase-2 AUTO-RECEIVE for the `msg` plugin (NO MCP, sidecar @D s15). A UNIX socket is a connection not a queue, so `/msg listen` drops messages when no one is bound. msg-arm fixes that: on SessionStart it (a) upserts self into `~/.sidecar/msg/roster.json`, (b) spawns a DETACHED capture daemon (`socat UNIX-LISTEN:<self>.sock,fork OPEN:<self>.inbox.log,append,create`, fallback `nc -lkU`) that OWNS the socket and appends every received line to `<self>.inbox.log` — idempotent via a `<self>.daemon.pid` pidfile + pgrep guard. On SessionStart + every UserPromptSubmit it reads `<self>.inbox.log` from the byte offset in `<self>.offset`, emits new lines as additionalContext (`📨 from X: ...`, capped ~20 lines), and advances the offset (resets to 0 on truncation). Self nick comes from `~/.sidecar/msg/self` (shared with the `msg` command plugin). NO opt-out switch (s11): gates only on real preconditions — nick set + a socket binary present; otherwise graceful exit 0. hexa-lang (`_msg_arm.hexa`, via `hexa run`). Hook half of msg; pairs with `msg` 0.2.0 whose `listen` verb retargets to `tail -f <self>.inbox.log` when the daemon owns the socket.
